question: Do you offer a BAA (Business Associate Agreement) for HIPAA compliance?
answer: Yes, we support HIPAA compliance and can sign a BAA. You can execute one directly via our DocuSign PowerForm here: https://na4.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=12d882a8-2414-419a-9d61-5b15a3d20c19&env=na4&acct=327087e3-0eb7-4ce0-b492-10daade58b39&v=2 Once countersigned by our team, a fully executed copy will be emailed to you.

question: What is your pricing? Do you have an Enterprise plan?
answer: We don't offer a separate Enterprise plan. Our pricing is entirely pay-as-you-go with automatic tiered discounts as your volume increases (starting at 10K+ hrs/mo). No upfront commitments are required either! If you're curious on what the tiers look like for a specific model, can you share your expected monthly transcription hours for those models?

question: Can we get volume discounts or custom pricing?
answer: Yes, we offer tiered volume discounts that apply automatically as your usage increases. These tiers start at 10,000 hours/mo for any given model. If you want to know more on what a specific tier looks like, can you please share what your expected monthly hours are and the models you're interested in?

question: Do you have a subcontractor BAA? We're not a covered entity but our customers are.
answer: Yes, our BAA covers business associates as well as covered entities. You can review our BAA FAQ here for details on how it applies to your situation: https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/faq/can-you-sign-a-baa If you're a business associate (not a covered entity), our standard BAA still applies and can be executed via the same Docusign process found here: https://na4.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?PowerFormId=12d882a8-2414-419a-9d61-5b15a3d20c19&env=na4&acct=327087e3-0eb7-4ce0-b492-10daade58b39&v=2

question: Do you use our data for model training? How do I opt out?
answer: You can opt out of data usage for model training at any time by emailing data-opt-out@assemblyai.com. Once opted out, your data is only processed to provide the transcription service and is not used for training, benchmarking, or product improvement. Our DPA (Data Processing Addendum) is automatically in effect when you create an account.

question: What is your data retention policy? Can we get zero retention?
answer: By default, transcription results are retained for a period after processing. We can enable a TTL (Time To Live) value for your account, though, where transcription artifacts are deleted on a set cadence as low as one hour. Our support team at support@assemblyai.com can help you set this up. Our best recommendation to minimize the time data is on our platform is to make DELETE requests each time you receive a transcript back from us. More information on how to perform that call can be found here: https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/api-reference/transcripts/delete

question: Where is your DPA (Data Processing Addendum)? We need a data privacy agreement.
answer: A DPA is automatically in effect when you create your AssemblyAI account and agree to our Terms of Service. You can review the complete DPA at any time here: https://www.assemblyai.com/legal/data-processing-addendum If you need specific amendments (e.g., restricting data use beyond what the standard DPA covers), we can work with you on a custom addendum.

question: Do you offer self-hosted or on-prem deployment?
answer: Yes, we can offer self-hosted deployment for Streaming transcription today, but not Async transcription. On-prem for Async transcription is on our roadmap for later this year. Can you please share what your expected monthly hours are and the models you're interested in?

question: Do you support real-time/streaming transcription?
answer: Yes, we offer both Async (pre-recorded) and Streaming (real-time) transcription APIs. Streaming supports real-time speech-to-text via WebSocket with low latency. Key features include automatic punctuation and multilingual support, as well as keyterms prompting to provide custom context to the model to further improve accuracy. You can learn more in this section of our documentation: https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/streaming

question: What languages do you support?
answer: The languages we support differ between Async and Streaming transcription, as well as the model you choose to use in each modality. Language support for each model we offer can be found here: https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/getting-started/models

question: Do you offer a bug bounty program?
answer: At this time, we do not have a formal public bug bounty program in place and do not offer compensation for unsolicited vulnerability claims; however, we are committed to the security of our systems and appreciate responsible disclosures. You are welcome, but not obligated, to share any identified vulnerabilities with us — if you are willing to do so, please send the details to security@assemblyai.com. We take security seriously, will independently investigate all findings, and will prioritize remediation for any issues deemed to be impactful.

question: Is Universal-3 Pro available streaming?
answer: Yes, our Universal-3-Pro model is available for both Async (pre-recorded) and Streaming (real-time) transcription. Check out this section of our documentation to learn more: https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/getting-started/universal-3-pro

question: Where can I find information on the Slam-1 model?
answer: The Slam-1 model is an older model that is no longer actively supported. For the best transcription results, we recommend using our latest models, such as Universal-3 Pro. You can find more information on our current model offerings here: https://www.assemblyai.com/docs/getting-started/models
